---
title: "References"
toc: true
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.kicker}
ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS · SOURCES · TEACHING ECOSYSTEMS
:::
# References
::: {.lead}
This curriculum did not grow in a vacuum. Everything worth keeping in
it came from somewhere else first.
:::
:::

The list below is not exhaustive — nothing that tries to cover an
entire field ever is — but it names the sources we leaned on hardest and
the teachers whose own openly available material shaped the pedagogy.
Every entry links to something you can read for free today. Where a book
is not open-access we have flagged it, and where a web resource exists in
parallel we have linked to that instead.

## Direct inspiration

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
Course structure
:::
### [Chi Zhang — *MF9130E*, University of Oslo](https://ocbe-uio.github.io/teaching_mf9130e/)
The OCBE introductory course that inspired the layout of this site. A
model of how to teach biostatistics transparently and openly, with every
lab reproducible from source. See also the
[GitHub repository](https://github.com/ocbe-uio/teaching_mf9130e).
:::

:::

## Comprehensive references

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
Clinical biostatistics
:::
### [Frank Harrell — *Biostatistics for Biomedical Research*](https://hbiostat.org/bbr/)
The single most opinionated, densest, most useful reference for applied
biostatistics in medicine. Required reading for anyone who wants to stop
being surprised by reviewers.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
Regression modelling
:::
### [Frank Harrell — *Regression Modeling Strategies*](https://hbiostat.org/rms/)
The companion to BBR. Spline fitting, calibration, validation, and the
`rms` package, all in one place.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
Statistical learning
:::
### [Hastie, Tibshirani, Witten, James — *ISLR2*](https://www.statlearning.com/)
Introduction to Statistical Learning with R — the definitive first book on
modern ML. The second edition adds deep-learning and survival chapters.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
Causal inference
:::
### [Hernán & Robins — *Causal Inference: What If*](https://www.hsph.harvard.edu/miguel-hernan/causal-inference-book/)
The canonical modern treatment. G-methods, IV, and the careful statement
of the questions that precede them.
:::

:::

## Teaching ecosystems

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
Bergen
:::
### [Biostats-R](https://biostats-r.github.io/biostats/)
A rich ecosystem of openly licensed courses in R for biosciences, hosted
by the University of Bergen.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
eR-Biostat
:::
### [eR-Biostat initiative](https://er-biostat.github.io/)
Community-maintained R-based materials for applied biostatistics.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
UBC Okanagan
:::
### [BIOL202 — Introduction to Biostatistics](https://ubco-biology.github.io/BIOL202/)
One of the clearest undergraduate introductions freely available online.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
Sheffield
:::
### [Introductory Biostatistics with R](https://dzchilds.github.io/stats-for-bio/)
Short, friendly, and opinionated. A good pairing with the first half of
Course 1.
:::

:::

## Pedagogy and reproducibility

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
Data-science teaching
:::
### [Mine Çetinkaya-Rundel — Data Science in a Box](https://datasciencebox.org/)
Every contemporary data-science course owes Mine a debt. Teach-the-teacher
material at its best.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
Quarto
:::
### [Quarto documentation](https://quarto.org/docs/guide/)
The reference manual for the publishing system this site is built on.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
Posit Education
:::
### [Posit Education](https://posit.co/about/posit-education/)
Openly licensed teaching resources from the makers of RStudio.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
`renv`
:::
### [renv: Project Environments for R](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/)
The tool that keeps this site reproducible across machines.
:::

:::

## Packages used

Every R package loaded by the labs is listed in `renv.lock` and re-exported
by `citation()` in the session-info block at the foot of each lab. If you
reuse any analysis here, cite the package authors alongside this site.

## Closing note

We are grateful to all the educators above for making their work openly
available. The entire point of a site like this one is that the next
version of it, wherever and by whomever it is written, will be better.
